task_completion.description: Verify that the YDBGui web interface is accessible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Firefox browser is open and displaying the YDBGui Dashboard.

task_completion.description: Verify that the VistA database is running and connected.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The YDBGui interface shows an active connection to the database, such as displaying the global directory tree or database status.

task_completion.description: Verify navigation to the Immunization Type global (^AUTNIMM).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The global '^AUTNIMM' is explicitly selected, searched, or opened within the YDBGui interface.

task_completion.description: Verify that vaccine definitions are visible in the ^AUTNIMM global.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Data nodes or values within ^AUTNIMM are displayed on screen, showing readable vaccine types, names, or definitions.

task_completion.description: Verify navigation to the Vaccination Event global (^AUPNVIMM).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The global '^AUPNVIMM' is explicitly selected, searched, or opened within the YDBGui interface.

task_completion.description: Verify that patient vaccination event records are visible in the ^AUPNVIMM global.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Data nodes or values within ^AUPNVIMM are displayed on screen, showing event details such as record numbers, dates, or pointers.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the YDBGui web interface to view the globals, rather than terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: The globals ^AUTNIMM and ^AUPNVIMM are viewed within the Firefox browser UI, not via terminal commands like 'mumps -dir' or 'ZWR'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the live YDBGui application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screen shows the actual YDBGui web interface with its standard layout, buttons, and navigation tree, rather than a text editor or static file.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not just write the global names into a text file to simulate completion.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no text editors open simply listing '^AUTNIMM' and '^AUPNVIMM' without actual database interaction.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The required interface is the YDBGui web interface running in Firefox. The target globals to review are exactly '^AUTNIMM' (Immunization Types) and '^AUPNVIMM' (Vaccination Events). The VLM should look for these specific global names and their expanded data nodes in the YDBGui interface.
